skill.name: ppxc-find-customers
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to find customer leads from short-video comments, generate customer-discovery keywords, review lead intent, query a customer pool, or prepare outreach scripts with PPXC.
skill.summary: Use this skill when the user wants to find customer leads from short-video comments, generate custom...
skill.author: yuanjian068yuan

security_audit.summary: The reported Ruby backtick findings are mostly false positives from Markdown fences and inline tool names. One setup block does instruct users to run an external MCP package with npx, and the file links to an external setup URL. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or weak cryptographic code was found in the reviewed file.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Package Execution in Setup Instructions
medium_findings.description: The setup block instructs users to run an external npm package through npx. This is legitimate for MCP installation, but it creates supply-chain and local execution risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command and package name are explicitly present in the MCP setup block. The file does not show malicious behavior, but npx execution depends on a remote package.

low_findings.title: External Setup URL Requires Trust Review
low_findings.description: The skill points users to an external setup URL. This may be normal documentation, but users must trust the linked domain before installing software.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is plainly present and related to setup. No evidence found that it exfiltrates data or uses deceptive text in this file.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged line is the skill description and does not contain cryptographic code, hashing functions, or encryption configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains descriptive frontmatter text only. No evidence found of weak cryptographic algorithms in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Findings Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Most reported backtick findings are Markdown code fences or inline tool names. They do not execute shell commands by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside Markdown examples and tool guidance. Only the npx setup command represents meaningful external execution risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Install Command for Remote Package
dangerous_patterns.description: The MCP configuration runs npx with a package name. This pattern can execute code from the npm ecosystem during setup.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The setup example directly specifies npx and an npm package. The risk is supply-chain execution rather than confirmed malicious intent.

content.user_title: Find Customer Leads from Video Comments
content.value_statement: Finding qualified buyers in short-video comments is time consuming and inconsistent. This skill guides PPXC workflows for keyword discovery, lead review, and outreach preparation.
actual_capabilities: Checks PPXC account readiness before lead discovery.
Lists products so lead searches match the right offer.
Generates customer-discovery keywords for short-video research.
Searches or analyzes comments for buying signals.
Reviews saved leads and ranks buying intent.
Prepares outreach scripts and support diagnostics.
limitations: Requires a working PPXC account and MCP connection.
Depends on PPXC data quality and available comment sources.
Does not guarantee that leads will convert into customers.
Setup requires trusting an external npm package and setup URL.

use_cases.title: Launch a Lead Search
use_cases.description: Find early buyer signals for a product by generating search angles and reviewing matching comments.

use_cases.title: Prioritize a Lead Pool
use_cases.description: Review saved PPXC leads and focus outreach on contacts with stronger purchase intent.

use_cases.title: Prepare Outreach Copy
use_cases.description: Turn customer signals into simple message scripts for follow-up and sales conversations.

prompt_templates.title: Start Lead Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Find customer leads for my product in PPXC. Check my account status first, then guide me through product selection.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Search Keywords
prompt_templates.prompt: Create customer-discovery keywords for this product and explain which buyer problems each keyword targets.
prompt_templates.scenario: Build search angles before comment analysis

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Comment Intent
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze short-video comments for this product and group leads by clear intent, possible intent, and low intent.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Outreach Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my PPXC lead pool, rank the best opportunities, and draft outreach scripts for each high-intent segment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced lead prioritization and action planning

output_examples.input: Find leads for a skincare product using short-video comments.
output_examples.output: The skill checks PPXC status, selects the skincare product, suggests intent keywords, reviews comments, and returns prioritized lead groups.

output_examples.input: Review my customer pool and prepare outreach.
output_examples.output: The skill summarizes high-intent leads, highlights common customer needs, and drafts concise follow-up messages.
The skill guides diagnostic export so account or MCP connection issues can be reviewed by support.

best_practices: Confirm PPXC status before running lead searches.
Select the exact product before generating keywords.
Review lead intent before sending outreach.
anti_patterns: Do not treat every comment as a qualified buyer.
Do not run outreach without checking context and consent.
Do not install the MCP package without reviewing the source.

faq.question: What does this skill help me do?
faq.answer: It helps find and review customer leads from short-video comments using PPXC workflows.

faq.question: Do I need a PPXC account?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow starts by checking PPXC status and login readiness.

faq.question: Can it create search keywords?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses PPXC guidance to suggest keywords for customer discovery.

faq.question: Can it write outreach scripts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can prepare scripts based on lead intent and customer signals.

faq.question: Does it guarantee sales results?
faq.answer: No. It helps organize discovery and outreach, but conversion depends on many factors.

faq.question: What security concern should I know?
faq.answer: Setup uses an external npm package and setup URL, so review them before installation.
